title: Ultimate Economy Mod
description: A Complete, Luxury economy and market system for Minecraft
body:
# Luxury Market Mod

Luxury Market Mod adds a fully dynamic economy system to Minecraft with live item prices, supply & demand, inflation, banking, player trading, and random market events.

Instead of static shop prices, the economy changes based on player activity and server-wide market conditions.

Sell too much iron? Prices drop.  
Players buying large amounts of food? Prices rise.  
As more wealth enters the server, the economy evolves over time.

## Features

- Dynamic supply & demand pricing
- Server-wide live market economy
- Banking and money transfer system
- Player marketplace and listings
- Inflation and wealth scaling
- Random market events
- Market drift and volatility systems
- Multiplayer-compatible economy syncing
- Server-side price calculations
- Long-term economy balancing

## Market Features

The market system allows players to:

- Buy and sell items
- View live price changes
- Track market trends
- Monitor item multipliers
- React to market events
- Trade with other players

## Economy Systems

Luxury Market Mod includes multiple systems working together to create a living economy:

- Supply & demand
- Inflation scaling
- Wealth scaling
- Volatility
- Market drift
- Snapshot comparisons
- Dynamic item multipliers

## Multiplayer Support

Designed for:

- SMP servers
- Economy servers
- Roleplay servers
- Civilization servers
- Long-term multiplayer worlds

Works in:

- Singleplayer
- LAN
- Dedicated servers

Required on both client and server.

## Commands

## Commands

### Player Commands
- `/bal`
- `/balance`
- `/pay <player> <amount>`
- `/uem` — opens the market GUI from anywhere
- `/marketprice <item>` — view price info for an item

### Admin Commands
- `/balanceof <player>`
- `/setbal <player> <amount>`
- `/marketevent <event>`
- `/marketdrift`
- `/marketprices`
- `/supplyset <item> <amount>`
- `/supplyget <item>`
- `/markettoggle <feature> <on|off>`
- `/tradeclear`

## Installation

1. Install Minecraft Forge
2. Place the mod in the `mods` folder
3. Install on both client and server
4. Launch the game

## Compatibility

- Forge
- Multiplayer compatible
- Dedicated server compatible
- Singleplayer compatible


## NOTES ## 

"**/markettoggle requests**" is currently not working as intended. Please disable by typing "**/markettoggle requests off**"

The Contribution Block and Treasury Block currently do not work as intended - this will be fixed in future updates.
